<!-- loading: on-demand (invoked via the Skill tool when needed) — generated by scripts/list-skills.sh --annotate; do not hand-edit -->

# Delivery conventions — the artifact path (PR M)

The merge gate ends at "commit verified"; this skill governs what happens next: turning a
green merge into an **immutable, traceable, signed artifact** that PR N's deploy path can
trust. Mechanism: `.github/workflows/build-provenance.yml` (bootstrap writes it; it chains
on `pipeline-ci` success via `workflow_run` and self-skips without a Dockerfile).

## Build + tag rules

- **Tag by commit SHA, never `latest`, in the deploy path.** The tag answers "exactly which
  reviewed commit is this?" — a mutable tag can't. Set the ECR repository to
  **immutable tags** so a tag can't be silently repointed.
- **Deploys reference the digest** (`repo@sha256:…`), not the tag — the digest is the
  artifact's identity; the tag is a human label.
- **Reproducibility posture:** pinned base image **by digest**, dependencies installed from
  the lockfile only, no network fetches at runtime-image build beyond the package install.
  (Bit-for-bit reproducibility is not claimed — the honest bar is "rebuildable from the
  commit with pinned inputs.")

## Sign + provenance (why three artifacts)

| Artifact | Produces | Answers |
|---|---|---|
| cosign signature (keyless OIDC) | `cosign sign` | "was this image pushed by OUR workflow?" — the identity is the workflow's OIDC token; **no long-lived keys exist to leak** |
| SBOM (CycloneDX, attested) | trivy image → `cosign attest` | "what exactly is inside it?" — feeds the weekly re-scan |
| SLSA build provenance | `actions/attest-build-provenance` | "which workflow, commit, and inputs built it?" |

**Verify-before-rollout (the rule PR N enforces):** the deploy step runs
`cosign verify` with `--certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com`
and a `--certificate-identity-regexp` pinned to this repo's workflow path **before any
rollout**; an unsigned or identity-mismatched image is refused. Signing without downstream
verification is theater — never ship the first without wiring the second.

## Container hardening (audit S4)

- **hadolint** lints the Dockerfile pre-build (errors fail the workflow).
- **dockle** checks the built image (fatal findings fail): non-root `USER`, no secrets in
  layers/env, no ADD-from-URL.
- Convention baseline (implementation writes it, these tools enforce it): non-root user,
  read-only root filesystem where the runtime allows, dropped capabilities, pinned base
  digest, multi-stage build so toolchains never ship.

## Continuous vulnerability management (audit S2)

Secure-at-ship ≠ stays-secure: today's clean pinned dep is next month's CVE.
- **Renovate or Dependabot** on every generated app repo — update PRs flow through the
  normal pipeline gates like any change.
- **Weekly scheduled re-scan of `main`** (OSV + Trivy image on the *latest shipped
  digest*), alerting on new High/Critical — the SBOM attested above is what makes the
  image-side re-scan cheap.

## Registry + credentials

ECR by default (AWS posture). Push happens via the **OIDC-assumed role** in
`configure-aws-credentials` — least-privilege (ECR push to the one repository), no stored
AWS keys, mirroring `iac-conventions`. The cosign identity is the same OIDC token; nothing
to rotate, nothing to leak.

## Deferred to PR N (do not improvise here)

The migration-executor sequence, canary-vs-blue/green rubric, rollback automation, and
feature-flag conventions are **PR N** decisions — already made in
`docs/environments-delivery-plan.md` (D2/D3); their sections land here when N builds.
